play_store_reviews.text: I hate this app more than any app I have ever used. It is so inefficient when you have hundreds of projects and you want to quickly find a job and check a task as completed. My work day will dramatically increase when someone can take this concept and make it efficient. I work deadline to deadline and long hours, and I don't have time to be in here searching for my job and where to close out a certain step for twenty minutes a day. I can't wait to never use this again.

wrike seems really good. HOWEVER, “As a solo trader, I rely on mobile as my primary device PLUS windows pc. Without time fields in the app, Wrike can’t serve as my full project hub.” - “This gap forces me to use a second app for reminders, which undermines Wrike’s value as an all‑in‑one solution.” - “Competitors like ClickUp and Asana already support times on mobile — Wrike risks losing mobile‑first users.” yes U can add time options in the web based website but I can NOT see the Android
Pretty good software. Great options to track and organize workflow, and I'm sure i'm just not using it to it's fullest extent. 2 years in and I'm still learning about it's features.

I was looking to move from paper planners to digital and wanted a nice productivity/management app. I read a few articles that mentioned Wrike and I wanted to test it out. Long story short, I didn't really like it and moved on. My issue isn't so much with the product as it is the overly-aggressive sales team. As soon as I registered, I received a flood of emails and PHONE CALLS about connecting to discuss the premium version! I fully deleted the account, unsubscribed from all email lists, and blocked the number. Within a week calls started again from a different number in a different state. This morning I had enough and finally answered the call. I asked them to stop calling ... and the guy just hung up after that! No apologies about being a annoyance or anything - just a click. Just a terrible experience overall. They made me regret ever downloading the app. You will have to REALLY want this product in order to tolerate the periphery experiences attached to using Wrike.

Happy user on web and Windows. Not a bad app but a bit tricky to navigate since the implementation of Spaces. Am finding it hard to find tasks and projects that are not (for historical reasons) not part of a space. Doesnt seem to show up in "browse"? Also, more importantly, there is no functionality to "quote" previous replies when responding (unlike the web and Windows GUI). Keep up the good work!
App keeps asking me for feedback in the inbox section. It takes half of the screen for this dumb thing and it shows every time, I open the inbox, although I close it every time.

I love the app. I know it's for team collab and mostly used for enterprises but I hope there is an option for an individual / solo person license. I have used it for few months as my personal PM / Goal / Task app. I thought I found the perfect app for me. Unfortunately I reached the max number of tasks. It would be great if there is a license for single users. It's just not practical to subscribe to a team license when I am just a single user. I'll look for alternative apps for now.

Hello, I needed the subscription based features urgently, but it took 8 hours to upgrade my membership. During that time, i looked elsewhere for the same features. But when I asked for a refund because I didn't use wrike, as I have already done what I needed using another platform, they refused to. Im giving them 1 star.

Why phone number needed for sign up?? Not enough that you can't even have a first look without signing up you have to introduce your phone number in order to do so. What's the reason? I hate that I have to sign up to every single app I just want to TRY if it's the right one for me. EDIT: Amazingly quick reaction to my post! I did the sign up again and was able to get access without being prompted for my phone number. Thank you and stay safe! (From 1 star to 4 stars)

I cant rate anywhere else so I will do it here: We have had Wrike for almost 2 years. At sign up we were told we could cancel any time. At our renewal our sales rep also didn't state that we could not cancel mid-subscription. The subscription agreement doesn't mention it can't be canceled and the billing/subscription part of my login doesn't include these details either. You have to dive dep into the terms portion of their website to get this. Just saying be careful as they aren't transparent

I hate Wrike. The company staff are rude and condescending, and care way more about their work than their customers. They replaced a feature with a new version, only the new version is a useless turd. The features of the old version have not been recreated even several years later, but they're forcing it through nonetheless. The sign of a great company is definitely one that prioritizes ease of work for their employees over ease of work for their customers. 💩

Constantly there are errors with time tracking. Timers on this app do not update based on what you do on the desktop app or browser. I've submitted many tickets and every response just says to open my app settings and clear out cache. You can't get the link from within the app to forward an email to a folder to make a task.

Wrike itself is a wonderful tool on the desktop version but the application is disabling if you are hoping to work from both or do much setting up via mobile. The lastest update changed the calendar which made my work week Tuesday - Saturday. I wish the app would get up to speed with the desktop functionality. Also, if a view is a available on the team plan on the desktop, it should also be available on the app - My assignments being the main thing here. Why of all things would this be omitted?

It has everything you need for project management. Its a flexible application, you can use it the way that works for you. It automatically migrates you to different kinds of productivity systems, like gantt chart, kanban board, table view, list and it even has a built in calendar. Works great, i would highly recommend it. I have tried the other apps out there, the az...,tre..., mon..., etc but so far this one works best for me, it has everything!

This app lacks way too much of the functionality of the service. I couldn't even find a way to view comments on a draft...... I may have just been missing it but in that case it's not clear how you're supposed to do that. This is already a service with some serious UI and organization issues and the app just makes it all worse. I'd much rather just use a browser on my phone to utilize right but they prevent you from doing that. This app is simply dreadful.

I upgraded my account from the trial to a paid account and it wiped all of my data. At that point I decided to move to ClickUp as they aren't in the habit of wiping people's data after the trial. I requested a refund of the $50ish payment that I made and the informed me they do not do refunds. Basically I paid $50 for them to wipe my data and push me to another software. Would NOT recommend. Go to ClickUp, Monday, Basecamp or anything else besides Wrike for a project management software.

The app is great. I like how it looks. Especially the courses teaching you how to use it. However, it's troublesome. When I open the phone app, and I click on something, the app closes. On the desktop app, when I try to create a folder and type in a letter, it opens another window. What's happening?

Being able to designate a Goal or Task as a Folder or Project is great. However, GANTT is not free and is cumbersome for paid versions. Free version is limited and Trello beats it by a mile. UI is not one of the most friendly. No Kanban. Would have been a great management tool if not for its limitations.

Do not use if you are a single user. The licenses can only be bought in multiples of 5. It truly is a great product, but do not try the trial version. You can't go back to the free version once you've seen what the full version can do. Hopefully one day they allow single license users. Until then leave it for the larger firms.

Great costumer support... Regarding the app... I wish it had more integrations with other apps (Like Asana and Todoist have) ... as well the interface could be much better....I like PM Apps that use icons with projects and different colours... makes work easier, As well check lists for the main tasks to check them off quickly.... etc.

Not the best UI, difficult to figure out. Software is starting to go in random directions with a button over there for some things and a button here for other things then others that only pop up after you beat a level boss. I'll just use a shared spreadsheet with pull down commands.

The wrike app is a constant disappointment. Laggy behavior on both my Pixel and Samsung devices. From video/image uploads taking hours (minutes in TEAMS, FB messenger, to crashes, and a poor GUI; this app is nothing but disappointment. My company uses this and we all have to work around the mobile app. Things are simple in browser simply fail in the mobile app. WHen transferring files its easier to use dropbox links onto the wrike task since photo/video uploads are so iffy. Overall Wrike is not bad but their mobile app is like a 1990's relic of failure.

Never uploads files, even with a good network connection. It also does not automatically upload them in the background when it can, so you have to sit there and watch it until it works. It just simply acts as if you never tried to upload a file to begin with.

Great app. Works just as well as the desktop version. Intuitive interface. Updates almost instantly when comments or files are uploaded by other collaborators to projects and tasks. I highly recommend it for anyone that already uses wrike online or on desktop.

Painfully slow app with some cool features, not super user friendly but I'm assuming anyone under 40 can figure it out. Also I dont see many apps with gantt charts that allow for hourly editing which could be something to consider. I can't see ever using this in a professional setting but it has been good so far for personal use.

When clicking on Gantt it does not show & gives me a comment to do something. If you want the USER to pay then you should make that clear right in the begin before he opens an account, give data and invests time !!! and not make him do 3,4 or more steps to get what he wants. Your instructions are too complex for a busy manager of today. First you steel data like email phone etc. and then you let him in the dark and he has to explore to get to the features. Customers are not stupid nowadays and such guidance and strategy annoys them and you are losing instead of gaining.

App is fine, but if you sign up for the free plan, sales people will start calling you every couple of days. I've never experienced anything like it before. It's obnoxious. I'll tell you one thing really bad for my productivity: regular repeated sales harassment calls that I have to screen.

I am wainting for the integration of the mobile app with Google Calendar (web version has it). Is there any plan for this? Also another big problem is licencing model: you cannot buy a single user licence. Only 5 users minimum. Otherwise it is a great product.

The app is completely unusable. I logged in once with a free trial. Then my company decided to get a subscription and it would be managed by our IT department. Now I can't get my new space to be visible on the app no matter what I do which tells me the company is doing their job managing and migrating user profiles.

Slow to the point of unusable I installed this, set up an account, and 10 minutes later it's still at a loading screen with the spinning wheel. Force closed app, opened again, still stuck at the spinning wheel. Useless.

This is one of the most effective and highest performing apps I have used. Our team works off of Apple/Android/Microsoft platforms and we have no issues. It has truly changed our teams' macro communication and efficiency. We highly recommend to anyone looking for additional oversight, transparency and efficiency for their team(s).
This app looks great. However, the free version, up to 5 users, limits the project to two levels. That's not free, that's "demo mode". Next level is $600/year. That prices me out...

It's ok, it has a fairly user friendly interface (as far as timeline/project management software goes) but it's not yet very feature-rich, several of the views are buggy, and the integration with other tools (especially Microsoft products) leaves a lot to be desired.

Great app! It seems amazing for businesses and offices, and with a little bit of tweaking, for personal use. It covers both basic and advanced needs, and is simple to use. My only complaint is that it is a little bit confusing and unoptimized for mobile. Despite that, it's nearly perfect in every way!

Just installed the app and I'm stuck at 'No notifications yet'. I can add tasks, or folders or anything. Anytime I try to navigate somewhere, I'm brought back to the landing page. I bet I'll move along and look for something better.

Best task/project management app available. Stay on top of tasks with real-time and email alerts, communicate with co-workers, give clear instructions and assign tasks to others, and follow-up with timers. Can't believe this is free. I just wish they implemented dark mode.

You have broken the ability to log in when using a custom rom. It comes up with the warning and then when you click continue anyway, it takes you back to the login screen and puts you in a loop. Please fix.
Login doesn't work. Checked, double checked and triple checked it's the same details I use to log in on desktop. Seems to be an issue with the app. Ended up giving up and deleting it.

Looking very good so far, except I might be missing something but I can't seem to move tasks, projects, folders etc from a location to within a folder. I believe this is a pretty core feature which should be implemented.

This is a great program. it has made communication in our company so much easier, and tracking workloads is easy. It's a very versatile program, which can be a slight drawback and a little overwhelming period, which takes some getting used to but well worth it.

As far as apps in the project and task management space go, this is among the best. It is well supported, frequently updated, and is fluid and fast. There are a few missing features compared to the web version and mobile time tracking could use better support. But overall, very good.

Good and stable mobile app, but I am getting confused by notifications on all levels... What's the difference between "activity" and "inbox"? I should spend time to learn how to use this maybe? And time what I don't have as Project Manager...
It is good for communication. However, I never receive notifications on my phone and I have toggled on all the notifications settings in the app and on my phone.
Impossible to use on mobile if you have more than five tasks, and projects. Wrike on desktop are great though.

Very easy to use....a lot more intuitive than other task manager systems I've tried and its so quick to add tasks and assign them...i actually prefer the app over the web version. Couldn't recommend this app more highly
says unknown error when i try to sign up and never goes past the sign in/up page

I wish they had a better way to show what each of my employees are up to, in a chart or calendar view. Their "workload" tab comes close, but it disappeared when our trial ended, and it's too expensive to get it back.
Customer support is good. They've been building up on the featuees accessible on Mobile so that's really good too! When I first accessed the app, there wasn't much I could do but now, I can!

Update on comments: I still love the platform! Its just heaven! Some reco in the mobile app however is that since it only has limited features, I wish I could +folder tagging, create projects/tasks from blueprints and quote from comments and the description box. So far those were my recent experience that I wanted to do on mobile but couldn't. I saw the same limitations on my ipad pro. I like the cheer-on feature like "great work!" Or "awesome!" Its a little detail that makes you feel good.

For some reason, starting a timer caused my phone to go into an infinite loop of locked screen (black), lock screen, brief view of home screen, and sometimes the Wrike app, then locked screen, lock screen, home screen, etc.

Currently using it to communicate maintenance issues around the facility to be completed and projects status. Our master account is on the PC (you need the pc to create tasks etc) then our technicians add comments and pictures then close when job is complete. Good tool when you have multiple departments looking for updates.

The interface is very clean and easy to use and yet offers all the functionality that you get from the desktop version. The app helps me communicate with my team and manage my work when I'm out of the office. Keep up the great work!

The ability that I have to check in on all tasks for me and my team is critical. I can look at any document and collaborate in real time. I can make adjustments just as if I was sitting in front of my computer.

Company has used Wrike since 2013 and I always feel like I'm learning something new. The system features and app have continually upgraded. Not everything is functional but enough is! The Android app is very handy to keep in touch or updated on the go.

Frustrating, focuses more on what you can't do than what you can. Can't change user color, order projects, or view anything but just tasks in the calendar view, not projects. I'm a visual organizer. I can't wrap my head around this app if I can't change the view of things.

A very good app for collaborating but of course it doesn't compete head to head with Microsoft project. Needs ability to easily duplicate tasks for the timeline. Needs better reporting support in general, and specifically for custom fields.

this is best App that can manage all my new business and servicing submission, thru this App I can no worry the submission status as it can be interact with my team and personal assistant, chat with specific new biz or servicing submission category, I like the concept! I had used it for many years... anyway still need to improve the integration with google calendar as not yet used friendly

Haven't even finished sign up and the UX is HORRIBLE. Broken pages, ads for different versions of the app jacking the sign up process, refusing perfectly good info with errors... Really bad for a paid product.

EDIT: Feature request fulfilled less than 4 weeks later. This is my second go at using the app and it is going much better. It's clean and not overly burdened by customization options (read: habit destroying weapons of mass destraction) and now supports DARK MODE 🕶️

The app is good enough. However, it falls short of our needs - the board view lacks swimlanes, and the dependency functionality disappears if you remove dates from a task. update April 2025: I lowered to 2 stars. they removed the "list" view which leaves only "table" view (saved "list" views were converted to"table"), which is in beta and not fully functional. So this is a straight loss of functionality. Why the rush???

Been sampling this app and as a PM starting out my private practice,I would say it's really proving to be promising.However check on 2 things 1.Your support centre personel, they do not pick calls and when they did they said they are more of a sales team and thus cannot assist,he promised to send a number for technical support and did not, check on this as support is crucial and 2.Still waiting for someone to get back to me on enterprise option of offering despite having put the request through
Good app and integration with outlook. Would consider a paid version for personal use if could buy a licence for individuals, not just for 5 or more
Horrible interface and app navigation. The desktop ver looks promising though, but I'm looking for an option that is mobile friendly. Uninstalling but hopefully improvements can be made.

Hard to make a mobile app with the same functionality as such a powerful web-based platform. So the app is good for referencing info and quick communication, but you'll want to WORK within the browser interface.
Lots of features but too expensive. Interface can sometimes be confusing. I recommend everybody Zoho Projects instead!
The best application for project management and personal task management. I have been using it since past 2-3 years and has helped me a lot and my team keep track of project timeline and activities.
Love the Wrike app! I use Wrike for work and the app is the perfect compliment. It lets me do everything I want to do while I'm out of the office and keeps me up-to-date. Very clean user interface.

I really love the app, the design and in a nutshell everything of this app. I'll be using it as long as it's free because in my country we can't pay for subscriptions etc. I wish it stays free. Thanks

The concept is solid, but i have to pay for basic things like creating sub-tasks. Also, I'm not fond of how the widgets are setup. Update: I've reduced your stars because someone at your company is still emailing me unasked & without my consent.

This is the best project planning app out there! This is so smooth to use, and literally offers all the features, thank for making the app free, please make it free forever, you will grow exponentially high in few months! Thank you so much Team!!

App doesnt accept login email addresses that have apostrophes in them as valid even though the website does. My work email has an apostrophe in it because my name has an apostrophe in it, therefore I can't log in with this app. (Omitting the apostrophe doesnt work)
Hard to get started. Not so intuitive. When you get passed the start it seems like incredible. You have just got to get past start

All tabs are empty even after I create folders, projects and tasks. Dashboards, Browse, My work - everything remains empty irrespective of things I create. How can I effectively manage a project if I have to type its name every time in Browse to see something?
Absolutely perfect for when I need to work ans be mobile. All the same features as the desktop but looks a lot better 😊
We've only used this app a short time, but we are also seeing an immediate increase in team unity and productivity. Wrike has a short learning curve but we havent been happier with any other PM tool.

Would be great if you come with package per income or something like that for individual use in Vietnam, Cambodia, Laos. We can see how much your product will improve our performance as we went through the trial, however, the cost is still too high.

Awful! I've been fighting with them for months about when to conduct Business and when not to. I work 9-5, Wrike contacts me between 11:30 PM, and 5:00 am. I'm so tired. I blocked them, spammed them, eliminated all options for contact, and deleted my email address, they contacted me at 11:52 PM to ask how their customer service was. Apparently I shall never sleep as long as my company continues to use this.
Best PM app. My team and I can collaborate in real time, attach docs, really like the spaces and reminders. We are more coordinated and efficient now than before.
The free version has all the necessary features. This appl provides good service and extras to guarantee your business success. This is a highly recommended appl
Can't download and install the app from play store. ** Update: The issue was with Play Store. I cleared data and cache from play store app, then it worked.

It made me create an account, but afterwords, the app wouldn't open, it is just a grey screen. I tried to cancel my account and make a new one, but to do that the app needs to open. How do I delete my old account or get the app to work on my mobile device?

Worse ,tried putting password and it even said it's incorrect but it's correct and did even confirm email but it purposely doesn't work after knowing your password thats what I'm assuming.will never use again..
App does not allow the same use as desktop. App should be easier to use, not harder.
Pretty great, there are a couple of key features that are missing from the Wrike environment, but overall one of the best project mgmt tools I've used.
Great app but I am unable to write comments as an approver for a task. I tap and hold as shown, comments screen opens but my keyboard does not open. Using android app on Samsung Galaxy s20+
As soon as I setup first project and went out of app to tell teammates to join, my project was gone. Very frustrating spending all that time to setup and then just gone.

Bad experience...Tried to make it to use with my team mates but even not understanding what's happening with this app not making invitation after added 1 person,please add some user notes to make it friendly
Didn't get to use the app or even see what it's purpose was because you have to sign in/create an account to move past the opening screen. No demo, no nothing.

Good app to be organized. But it can improve. Some of the things you can add: - Have the possibility of change due date of multiple selected tasks. - Android app doesn't have the possibility of compress tasks by date in my To-do section.

Missing features that unfortunately cause me to have to stick to desktop version :( -- 1. inability to snooze 2. inbox doesn't show the same tasks as it does on desktop 3. inability to choose which calendar the task displays on
I've set up projects and task, as well as deleted the intro task they offer for a tutorial. However, none of that shows up in the app. I've now deleted the app.

So complicated! I watched the "settling in" video and it's talking about "your home page" but I couldn't even find the homepage. Trying to improve my time management; I don't want a product I have to take a lengthy course to learn. Uninstalled

This is pretty darn incredible for project and task management. My wrike journey is only just beginning... But I can see this spreading right across my department and business. This app has it all! Collaboration at its heart, advanced reporting capabilities, enforced accountability for tasks and the UI is a dream. Well done Wrike
The app its focused in tasks not in projects. I dont find a easy way to stop the email notifications. There are options that you could do only via web browser, not in app.

Brian or Wrike. Thanks for being real. Not just a screen. I'll be in touch. I been needing not so much a quick and easy system, but one that pushes me to produce more. I'll be on Wrike like clock work. Thanks again you guys and being ready to answer any question!

All tabs are empty even after I create folders, projects and tasks. How can l effectively manage a project if I have to type its name every time in 'Browse' to see something? 😏 Totally waste, unless there's a bug in the App!
Wrike on web is an amazing platform. However the app has some constraints. Especially in terms of navigation and user experience. Had it have same kind of UX like web, it would have been amazing.
Interesting productivity app. With a lot to feature to help you organise your projects. It would be great if tasks for each day were organise into an easily accessible simple to do list.
Great tool for getting and staying organized. Very easy to use. Support is at the top of their game. Special shout out to Ariel White!! Honest, upfront and very helpful!!!

Wrike is the best project management software ever. Flexible that an entire organization can benefit from using it. They also have great training and support. The mobile app allows you to not miss a beat on your tasks and projects.
I find it strange that you can set task priority from Android devices! It's a simple task had it been provided! Apart from this, great and helpful app to organize busy work
The description says work anywhere in the world.. I created an account and installed the app, then when trying to use it I got a message stating the service is not allowed in my country.
2nd to none. Nothing more powerful, versatile or intuitive. Trying to consider a similar app, not a single one comes to mind...
Extremely good program for work flow and project management. App can be harder to navigate then the computer version, but still very user friendly.
Tremendous functionality and always evolving based on user feedback. Totally serves the basic Task Management needs of our small team with the free version!
Great app! Using on Galaxy S8+ currently. iPhone version is a different story, though. Many key features missing makes the app near useless on anything but Android.

so far so helpful... discussions and files all in one place rather than sifting through long email threads... occassionally, the widget shows old messages somehow skipping the most up to date exchange... 🤔
Simple platform, easy to setup and use, perfect for tracking multiple projects as well as assigning tasks to team members and tracking their progress.

Almost good enough, this needs a table view for viewing the entire project tasks in one screen. It's just too easy to get lost when having to flip deeper in screens for every task hierarchy. It's a usability issue, otherwise what would be a nearly complete package

Poor user experience. It's hard to find tasks across multiple projects. Task view is overly complicated and needs visual cleaning. For whatever reason sub-tasks don't show on the board, just the main patent task does which is making it very hard to track work and action on time. Not following the best user experience practices in all aspects. I love wrike but overall application did no improve since it originally launched.
I set up my account with my phone number so it'd be easy to log in, not so you could call me every 3 days to see how I'm liking the app.

while the core application provides the basic usage needs for my team, the overall user experience could use some tweaking. Message engagement, Gantt and timeline integration, notifications, dashboards, task assignee/creater engagement... etc...
this phone app is awful. I can barely find anything and it looks nothing like the desktop app. very disappointed. cant even clean out my folders or do a mass edit
They harass you constantly, i have received multiple phone calls each day for weeks now. Would never recommend this app to anyone.

VERY powerful. Very hardworking App. Free and it works offline just as fast negotiating all corners in Tasks, Collaborations, and Project Management We are now focusing on Price | otherwise highly recommended!!
There is no functionality, and it is difficult to locate any features. I will be uninstalling the application. It is not unexpected, considering the website is not mobile-friendly.
I like how deep it is. There is so much you can do in the mobile app. They didn't make it so simple that it sacrificed top many desktop features.

Almost having All features required for Project management but App UI can be change to look more attractive , i have checked many similar apps That Has nice UI but all of them Lack of features .....Kindly Create Some New Themes.
Long time user here. Description inputs aren't Saving in app However appears to work on the web version. Need help from someone that isn't AI

Really disappointed with the support. Our company decided not to renew our subscription but Wrike just automatically renewed without any notification. We requested for the company to consider refund but they decided to refuse our request and charges us for this unearned revenue during difficult period. Wrike staff only refers to T&C without sympathy, the support is really different when they tried to pitch us. Other CRM does offer project management function these days.
Helps keep work groups working. a bit too many email notifications but it has really helped me keep in contact and track what my team is working on.

Terrible for freelance. Their team plan for the aforementioned is useless and heavily gimped. Proofing tool only available on Business plan which has minimum of 5 seats totalling $1,488 a year with no monthly payment option.

Unable to duplicate tasks, make bulk changes, print from app, nor fully delete collaborators using the mobile app. Mobile apps should be designed for users to work smarter not harder. Wrike PC - 5 Stars | Wrike Mobile - 2.5 Stars
This app is good but why it takes so much time for uploading videos in project and it does not contain any reminder for tasks to be done.

For someone like me who procrastinates and whose mind wanders around from one idea or task to other, the app has given me great tool to stay focused and get my team also to be focused on projects and ideas
Everythings's great with Wrike. Time monitoring, conversing with collegues, tagging them for updates, respondin on their msgs, attaching files. 🥳 5 stars guys! Keep it great!
Logs me out once weekly at least. Switches me over to some team I never joined. Annoying to sign back in. A huge hassle. Find another app.
Could not easily create projects and tasks for a gantt chart. Then they called me and left me a voicemail?? Complete overreach.
Need an app that serves as accountability partner to get your work done? Wrike is your go to app. It's experience is awesome.
Nice apps. Better than the other similar apps i think. But, you need to make a simple navigation for mobile.
makes the office work super easy and handy. the ease if tagging team mates and regular notifications ensures nothing is missed.
Please make it available on Google chromecast with Google TV. It would be really cool to have a Dashboard on a big screen like on TV.
Awesome app. Simple and intuitive UI/UX, rich in features in the free version. Runs smooth as a butter. Still exploring it, but so far I like what I see.
Slow, far from a task manager easy app. No way to filter tasks by assigned user. No widgets optimized
The app so far is working great. The whole program generally is perfect so far for my small team of 4. Great invention.
Wish that I could set a default workspace for when I open the app, but otherwise great.
Cannot delete my accont from mobile app. And cannot login to help center to write deletion request. Not so user friendly approach.
Read the terms of service for extra charges and company obligations. Unbelievable & a shame could have been a useful app.
Data loss. Even worse, we have a backup and they won't let us restore from it! Avoid at all costs!
Awesome cloud project management app for work. Web/desktop app is easier to navigate though. Dark theme basically breaks the interface.

very helpful when away from my desk to converse on projects with my team. always new functionality being added, generally feels complete enough but could use some ui/ux upgrades to make managing projects a little more intuitive on mobile.
hated this app. difficult to use. not at all intuitive. made more work for me, not less. just awful. the day I uninstalled I actually dsnced.
Easy to use, tracking project progress, and self management features.
really great app, but i have a team less than 5 users, so it becomes expensive forn use it please make more flexible pricing
Best project Management mobile app I have used so far. Organizing projects and tasks in folders is very cool.
Clean and efficient app. Obviously web version is better, but this keeps our team agile, mobile and on target for projects.
Not bad at all. I run into issues tracking my latest tasks and navigating to projects but thats more about the UI
One of the best free project management tools i ever used. This is feature loaded and easy to use.
